governanceNotes: Use this file as a lightweight Phase 2 accessibility and usability review record.
Repeat review after the assistant is hosted and embedded in the real Moodle pilot course.
Do not store student names, student IDs, grades, or disability accommodation details in this file.

checks.label: Responsible-use notice is keyboard accessible
checks.evidence: Dismiss button is visible and should be checked with keyboard-only navigation in Moodle.
checks.recommendedAction: Confirm Tab reaches the dismiss button and Enter or Space activates it.

checks.label: Chat input and starter prompts are reachable
checks.evidence: Chat input and starter prompts are rendered as standard focusable controls.
checks.recommendedAction: Confirm Tab order inside /embed after the app is placed in Moodle.

checks.label: Loading, error, and feedback status messages are announced
checks.evidence: The interface includes status messaging, but manual screen-reader review is still required.
checks.recommendedAction: Run a short VoiceOver test for loading, error, and feedback saved states.

checks.label: Embedded assistant fits in Moodle without blocking the input
checks.evidence: Requires confirmation inside the controlled FYEC100 Moodle course shell.
checks.recommendedAction: Review the assistant in Moodle page, block, drawer, or modal placement before pilot use.

checks.label: Students can understand assistant limits and academic integrity guidance
checks.evidence: Student-facing responsible-use and academic integrity language is visible.
checks.recommendedAction: Ask a small pilot group to confirm the language is clear before wider rollout.

checks.label: Fallback and escalation guidance is understandable
checks.evidence: Fallback guidance directs students to lecturer, LMS, or administrator support paths.
checks.recommendedAction: Confirm fallback wording during the dry-run and update the knowledge base if students remain unsure.
